commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a separate Codex app-server gate so server-scoped `toolsAllow` globs can enable user MCP server attachment while leaving native Code Mode gated on a full wildcard.

PR surface: Source +55, Tests +48. Total +103 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level. Current main suppresses user MCP projection when `toolsAllow` is a server-scoped glob because startup passes `userMcpServersEnabled` from the native Code Mode gate, which only accepts absent or wildcard allowlists.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor or human follow-up is needed to repair the MCP projection boundary and add real behavior proof; ClawSweeper repair should not try to supply contributor-side runtime proof for this external PR.
